points.label: Balloon landing intro (forced replay)
points.summary: NEW: the Se/rver first-visit intro (F-16). ?anim=1 forces a replay through every gate. PASS: the logo vortex (gold/blue pinwheels) untwists into WIRE balloons seen from above, the camera then drops a FULL 180° — past the side view and underneath, twisting sideways on the way down — clouds swish upward past the view, color floods back into FIVE balloons (same shape, different sizes), baskets and ropes hang in, and it ENDS looking up from below with warm burner glows in the mouths; whole thing ~4 s and noticeably brisker than /cure's umbrella intro; a tap anywhere skips instantly and the app underneath works. FAIL: asterisk-looking balloons (unfilled gores), a stuck full-screen canvas, or the intro replaying on a plain visit without ?anim=1. Please confirm twice: once in a normal tab, once in the iOS PWA.

points.label: Blue ✓ waiting symbols on research steps
points.summary: CHANGED: the blue tier's loading indicators are now BALLOONS (the umbrella spinner stays on /cure only). Send the prefilled question with web search ON. PASS: while researching, each step's spinner is a small WIRE balloon boomeranging (it must NEVER show a colored balloon while still running); when a step completes it speed-runs into a colored gold-and-blue balloon, holds a beat, then folds into a BLUE check mark — and the big typing indicator plays the same finale before the answer reveals. FAIL: any PINK check mark anywhere on /rver, a spinner that vanishes with a popped-in check (no finale), or a spinner still boomeranging after the answer landed. If anything is off, please attach the research-debug JSON in the note.

actions.text: What are the main approaches to making privacy properties of AI assistants independently verifiable?

points.label: Guide balloon: flare + pennant per task, swish on new chat
points.summary: The corner guide balloon (bottom-right, above the composer, hovering among small clouds). Send the prefilled question and let it finish. PASS: the moment the reply completes, the guide's burner FLARES gold, it climbs a notch, a small pennant unfurls under the basket, and clouds streak DOWNWARD past it; a second completed task adds a second pennant and another notch. Then press the new-chat button: the pennants clear and clouds swish PAST it sideways. It must never intercept taps (everything under it stays clickable). FAIL: no flare/pennant on completion, the balloon drifting over UI it blocks, or it vanishing entirely. Please confirm twice: once in a normal tab, once in the iOS PWA.

actions.text: Give me a short overview of how end-to-end encryption is typically audited.

points.label: Per-task channel: sandbox step wears the UMBRELLA on Se/rver
points.summary: NEW granular symbol grammar: a step that runs entirely on-device wears Se/cure's umbrella even on the blue tier. Turn the sandbox knob ON (Settings → the experimental Linux sandbox), then send the prefilled request. PASS: the sandbox step's waiting symbol is the PINK UMBRELLA spinner (clearly different from the balloon spinners on the other steps), and on completion it folds into the tier's plain BLUE check mark like every other step. FAIL: the sandbox step showing a balloon, or a pink check mark appearing on /rver.

actions.text: Use the shell to count the lines in /etc/os-release and tell me the distro name.

points.label: Se/cure: online steps end as tappable ℹ notices, local ones as pink ✓
points.summary: NEW on /cure (needs a provider key, or a borrowed research-space link): send any research question. PASS: steps that go ONLINE (analyzing/harvesting/writing/reviewing — your provider; any web search) show the BALLOON spinner and complete into a small blue ℹ instead of the pink check; tapping the ℹ opens a bubble that says exactly what that step sent and where (e.g. 'sent to OpenAI directly from your browser on your own API key — the DeepResearch.Se server was not involved'), and tapping anywhere else closes it; steps that stay on-device (the sandbox, a clarify question) keep the umbrella spinner and the pink check. FAIL: a pink check on a step that called a provider, an ℹ that opens nothing, or a bubble that won't dismiss. Please read one bubble fully and judge whether the disclosure is accurate and understandable — that text is the feature.
